task_completion.description: Open the source CSV file in Jamovi
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi application window is open and displays the data from 'NeuroticiIndex.csv', showing columns like N1 and N2.
A new column named 'N1_Binary' is visible in the Jamovi spreadsheet. The formula bar may show 'IF(N1 > 3, 1, 0)' and the column should contain binary values (0 and 1).
A new column named 'N2_Binary' is visible in the Jamovi spreadsheet. The formula bar may show 'IF(N2 > 3, 1, 0)' and the column should contain binary values (0 and 1).

task_completion.description: Perform McNemar's test comparing the two binary variables
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi results panel on the right displays a 'Paired Samples' or 'McNemar test' contingency table and statistical results involving 'N1_Binary' and 'N2_Binary'.

task_completion.description: Save the Jamovi project to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi window title bar shows 'NeuroticismMcNemar.omv' or a save dialog is visible confirming the file is being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/NeuroticismMcNemar.omv'.

task_completion.description: Record the resulting p-value in the text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output shows the file 'mcnemar_results.txt' containing the text 'p_value: < .001' (or a similarly extremely small value derived from the test).

integrity.description: The agent used the Jamovi graphical user interface to perform the analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Jamovi GUI (menus, formula editor, results panel) rather than just terminal commands executing R/Python scripts.

integrity.description: The binary variables were created using Jamovi's compute function
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'N1_Binary' and 'N2_Binary' columns should appear as computed variables in Jamovi (often indicated by an 'fx' icon or visible formula), not just manually typed data.

integrity.description: The recorded p-value matches the actual Jamovi output
integrity.visual_evidence: The p-value written to 'mcnemar_results.txt' must match the p-value displayed in the Jamovi results panel.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct p-value for the McNemar test on this dataset is extremely small (exact: ~3.34e-75, chi-squared: ~8.79e-69). In Jamovi, this will typically be displayed as '< .001'. The VLM should verify that the recorded p-value in the text file reflects this result (e.g., 'p_value: < .001').
